target.title: fix(amazon-bedrock-mantle): gate implicit discovery on AWS creds presence (#67288)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #67288. The `amazon-bedrock-mantle` plugin ran implicit Mantle provider discovery on every catalog refresh even when the user had no AWS credentials configured, emitting `[bedrock-mantle-discovery] Mantle IAM token generation unavailable` log lines on every request and attempting IAM bearer-token generation through `@aws/bedrock-token-generator` that was guaranteed to fail. The user-visible symptom is constant log noise on Raspberry Pi installs with no AWS env vars set.

The sibling `amazon-bedrock` plugin already has the gate: at `extensions/amazon-bedrock/discovery.ts:596`, implicit discovery short-circuits when `discovery.enabled !== true && !hasAwsCreds`. The mantle plugin had the `enabled === false` check but never the no-creds skip path.

## Fix

Mirror amazon-bedrock's gate in `resolveImplicitMantleProvider`:

```ts
const enabled = params.pluginConfig?.discovery?.enabled;
if (enabled === false) return null;
if (enabled !== true && !explicitBearerToken && !hasAwsSdkCreds(env)) return null;
```

Adds a small `hasAwsSdkCreds` helper that delegates to `resolveAwsSdkEnvVarName` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-auth-runtime` (same helper bedrock uses via `resolveBedrockConfigApiKey`). Also exports `MantleDiscoveryConfig` so the type is consumable from the registration site.

## Boundary

- Extension-local fix (`extensions/amazon-bedrock-mantle/`); no core or schema changes.
- No new config keys. Users who explicitly set `plugins.entries.amazon-bedrock-mantle.config.discovery.enabled: true` still get implicit discovery (and the IAM token generation attempt) even without AWS creds — opt-in for cases where the bearer token is sourced through some out-of-band mechanism.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Users running OpenClaw on hosts with no AWS credentials (Raspberry Pi, default Docker installs) reported continuous `[bedrock-mantle-discovery] Mantle IAM token generation unavailable` log lines on every request. The mantle plugin's `resolveImplicitMantleProvider` would attempt `generateBearerTokenFromIam(...)` even when neither `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK` nor SDK creds were available, with the failed lookup logged on every catalog refresh. The amazon-bedrock plugin has a discovery.enabled gate plus a no-creds skip path; mantle was missing the no-creds skip.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at HEAD `995ed29231` on Node 22 / Darwin 25.2.0, exercising the production `resolveImplicitMantleProvider(...)` (`extensions/amazon-bedrock-mantle/discovery.ts`) against a real `process.env` shape (empty, with no AWS creds) and against the SDK-creds-aware `resolveAwsSdkEnvVarName` helper from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-auth-runtime`. No mocks of the resolver under test.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm test extensions/amazon-bedrock-mantle/discovery.test.ts` from the repository root, compiling the production source via tsgo and driving the live `resolveImplicitMantleProvider` against (a) empty env (no AWS creds) and (b) explicit `pluginConfig.discovery.enabled: false` with AWS bearer token set.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured terminal output from the `pnpm` invocation above on this exact branch:

  ```
   RUN  v4.1.6 /Users/shubh-trips/Documents/personal-project/open-source/openclaw

   ✓ |extension-providers| skips implicit discovery entirely when no AWS creds are present (#67288)
   ✓ |extension-providers| honors pluginConfig.discovery.enabled=false even when AWS creds are present (#67288)

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  29 passed (29)
     Duration  1.14s

  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.58s
  ```

  Runtime assertions captured by the live resolver runs: (a) with empty env (no `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK`, no SDK creds env), the resolver returns `null` AND the `tokenProviderFactory` spy is NOT called — proving we short-circuit before IAM token generation; (b) with `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK` and `AWS_REGION` set but `pluginConfig.discovery.enabled: false`, the resolver still returns `null` and the token factory is not invoked — proving the explicit-disable path wins over creds presence; (c) the 27 existing discovery tests all continue to pass, including the previously-passing "returns null when no auth is available" case (the old behavior path that the user complained about — now it returns null *without* the noisy log).
- **Observed result after fix:** Installs without AWS credentials no longer see the per-request "Mantle IAM token generation unavailable" log lines and no longer attempt IAM bearer-token generation. The behavior for installs with `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK` set or with full SDK creds is unchanged. The behavior for explicit `discovery.enabled: false` is unchanged.
- **What was not tested:** End-to-end behavior on an actual ARM64 Raspberry Pi (the bug's reported environment) — the regression test exercises the contract layer that the per-request catalog refresh consumes, which is what produced the user-visible log spam. Downstream catalog/registration flow is unchanged.

## Risk

- **Surface:** one bundled extension (`amazon-bedrock-mantle`), discovery-only gate.
- **Backwards compat:** installs that depend on implicit Mantle discovery without AWS creds env vars need to opt in via `plugins.entries.amazon-bedrock-mantle.config.discovery.enabled: true`. The original bug report explicitly disabled mantle to work around the noise, so the documented expected migration is: either set the bearer token / SDK creds, OR set `discovery.enabled: true` to opt in to the legacy noisy behavior.

## Closes

Fixes #67288

target.title: fix(tts): sanitize summarized speech text
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90364.

TTS summarization now sanitizes summary-model output before returning text for speech:

- strips assistant/control scaffolding, echoed summary prompt instructions, narrow leading prompt preambles, and exact or standalone echoed `<text_to_summarize>` blocks
- preserves legitimate first-person summaries, user-summary prose, and inline prompt-tag mentions
- keeps cleanup in `summarizeText`, before auto-TTS assigns `summary.summary` to audio text

## Root Cause

`src/tts/tts-core.ts` joined `TextContent` blocks from `completeSimple` and returned the raw string as `summary.summary`. Auto-TTS then used that value directly as `textForAudio`, so reasoning tags, control tokens, prompt echo, or echoed source text could be spoken.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Long-response TTS summaries no longer pass hidden reasoning/control scaffolding, echoed prompt text, exact or standalone echoed source prompt blocks, or control tokens through to the audio text candidate. Regression cases also cover preserving first-person `key points` prose, preserving inline `<text_to_summarize>` mentions, stripping joined later-block prompt echoes, and stripping bare `The user asked me to summarize.` prompt echoes.

Real environment tested: Dedicated local macOS OpenClaw source checkout on branch `codex/fix-tts-summary-sanitization` at commit `4b446ccdfc`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-tts-summary-proof.ts`

Evidence after fix:

```console
OpenClaw TTS summary proof
sanitizedSummary="Concise audible summary."
sanitizedOutputLength=24
targetLength=120
leakedForbiddenTokens=none
firstPersonSummary="I need to keep the key points from today's review. The release moves to Friday."
firstPersonPreserved=yes
inlineTagSummary="The docs mention `<text_to_summarize>` as a literal prompt marker. The release moves to Friday."
inlineTagPreserved=yes
joinedPromptSummary="Concise audible summary."
bareUserPromptSummary="Deployment was delayed until Friday."
audioText=Concise audible summary.
```

Observed result after fix: The production `summarizeText` cleanup path returned only `Concise audible summary.` for a scaffolded summary-model response, preserved the first-person and inline-tag summaries, stripped the joined prompt-block echo, and stripped the bare user-summary prompt echo.

What was not tested: Live Microsoft/MiniMax audio playback was not run because provider credentials and audio playback setup were not available in this local checkout.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tts/tts-core.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/tts.contract.test.ts packages/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-tts-summary-proof.ts`

Focused verification passed across 3 Vitest shards: 2 unit-fast files / 165 tests, 2 unit files / 44 tests, and 1 plugin-contract file / 41 tests.

target.title: fix(heartbeat): debounce requests-in-flight retries
target.body:
Fixes #40611.

## What changed

- Adds a requests-in-flight heartbeat retry delay of 60 seconds.
- Keeps the existing 1 second retry delay for other retryable busy skips such as cron-in-progress and lanes-busy.
- Updates heartbeat wake and scheduler tests to cover the new requests-in-flight retry behavior.

## Behavior proof

Setup:

```bash
git worktree add -b tui-40611-heartbeat-busy-retry /Users/allahyar/Documents/fastino-tasks/openclaw-tui-40611 origin/main
cd /Users/allahyar/Documents/fastino-tasks/openclaw-tui-40611
```

Before this change, requests-in-flight skips used the shared wake retry delay:

```ts
const DEFAULT_RETRY_MS = 1_000;
...
schedule(DEFAULT_RETRY_MS, "retry");
```

After this change, requests-in-flight waits 60 seconds while other busy skips still use the default retry delay:

```ts
const REQUESTS_IN_FLIGHT_RETRY_MS = 60_000;
...
const retryDelayMs =
  res.reason === HEARTBEAT_SKIP_REQUESTS_IN_FLIGHT
    ? REQUESTS_IN_FLIGHT_RETRY_MS
    : DEFAULT_RETRY_MS;
schedule(retryDelayMs, "retry");
```

The new `heartbeat-wake` test proves a requests-in-flight skip does not retry after the old 1 second path and does retry after the new 60 second path.

## Validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/heartbeat-wake.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.scheduler.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.skips-busy-session-lane.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  57 passed (57)
```

```bash
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/heartbeat-wake.ts src/infra/heartbeat-wake.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.scheduler.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.skips-busy-session-lane.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
All matched files use the correct format.
```

```bash
git diff --check
```

Result: no output.

Attempted the requested broader changed-files check:

```bash
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"
pnpm check:changed
```

Both failed before running checks because the local `crabbox` binary failed its own basic `--version`/`--help` sanity checks:

```text
[crabbox] selected binary failed basic --version/--help sanity checks
```

## Platform tested

- macOS, local OpenClaw checkout.

## Limitations

- I did not run a live Telegram/Feishu/WebChat stall reproduction.
- This is source/test-backed validation for the heartbeat retry path.

target.title: fix(agents): avoid inviting provider swaps in model alias guidance
target.body:
## Summary
- stop telling the model to prefer aliases when a full provider/model override is already present
- clarify that aliases are only shortcuts for unqualified model requests
- update the prompt test to lock in the exact-ref guidance

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: with model aliases configured, the generated system prompt was telling the model to prefer an alias even when an exact `provider/model` override was already specified.

Real environment tested: a real OpenClaw checkout with the alias config below, comparing the generated system prompt on current `main` versus this branch.

Config used:
```yaml
agents:
  defaults:
    models:
      github-copilot/gpt-4.1:
        alias: copilot-4.1
      openai/gpt-4.1:
        alias: openai-4.1
```

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. generated the system prompt with the config above on current `main`
2. generated it again on this branch
3. compared the model-alias guidance line and alias entries

Evidence after fix: copied live output from the generated prompt comparison.
Current `main` says: "Prefer aliases when specifying model overrides; full provider/model is also accepted." It then lists `copilot-4.1: github-copilot/gpt-4.1` and `openai-4.1: openai/gpt-4.1`.
This branch says: "Use exact provider/model strings verbatim when one is specified. Aliases are shortcuts for unqualified model requests." It then lists the same alias mappings.

Observed result after fix: the model-facing prompt no longer tells the model to rewrite an explicit `provider/model` override into an alias.

What was not tested: I did not run a fully deterministic live provider swap scenario because the downstream behavior depends on model choices after reading the prompt; the proof here is the direct before/after prompt output that drives that behavior.

## Testing
- `pnpm vitest src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`

Closes #61949

target.title: fix(voice-call): track Twilio streams after connect
target.body:
Fixes #81122.

## What changed

- Track a Twilio call as active only when the media stream actually connects via `registerCallStream`.
- Stop marking a call active when TwiML is merely generated.
- Update existing queue/cleanup tests to explicitly register connected streams.
- Add regression coverage for the case where an inbound call receives stream TwiML but never opens a media stream.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Twilio inbound queueing no longer treats generated stream TwiML as an active media stream. If the first inbound call receives streaming TwiML but never opens a WebSocket media stream, a later inbound call receives streaming TwiML instead of being sent to `hold-queue`.

Real environment tested: macOS arm64 local OpenClaw checkout. Base proof used clean `origin/main` at `1a3ce7c2` with only the new regression-test diff applied in `/private/tmp/openclaw-81122-before-9071`. Fix proof used branch `fastino-81122-twilio-hold-music` at `22575a9f`. Package manager was pnpm v11.2.2.

Exact steps or command run after the patch: Ran the Twilio provider command on the patched branch: `pnpm test extensions/voice-call/src/providers/twilio.test.ts --reporter verbose`. Also ran `git diff --check`, `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/voice-call/src/providers/twilio.ts extensions/voice-call/src/providers/twilio.test.ts`, and `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/voice-call/src/providers/twilio.ts extensions/voice-call/src/providers/twilio.test.ts --report-unused-disable-directives-severity error`. For before evidence, I created a clean `origin/main` worktree and applied only the new regression-test diff, then ran the same Twilio provider command.

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output and TwiML payload from the local OpenClaw provider path. Before fix, the second inbound call produced hold queue TwiML instead of a stream URL:

```text
$ pnpm test extensions/voice-call/src/providers/twilio.test.ts --reporter verbose
FAIL |extension-voice-call| extensions/voice-call/src/providers/twilio.test.ts > TwilioProvider > does not block subsequent call when first call never opens a media stream
AssertionError: expected ... to contain wss://example.ngrok.app/voice/stream

Received:
<Response>
  <Say voice="alice">Please hold while we connect you.</Say>
  <Enqueue waitUrl="/voice/hold-music">hold-queue</Enqueue>
</Response>

Test Files 1 failed (1)
Tests 1 failed | 24 passed (25)
```

After fix, the same provider path returned streaming TwiML for the second inbound call; the regression checks for `wss://example.ngrok.app/voice/stream`, `<Connect>`, and no `hold-queue` payload:

```text
$ pnpm test extensions/voice-call/src/providers/twilio.test.ts --reporter verbose
✓ |extension-voice-call| extensions/voice-call/src/providers/twilio.test.ts > TwilioProvider > does not block subsequent call when first call never opens a media stream 0ms
Test Files 1 passed (1)
Tests 25 passed (25)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 7.98s

$ git diff --check
# passed with no output

$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/voice-call/src/providers/twilio.ts extensions/voice-call/src/providers/twilio.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

$ pnpm exec oxlint extensions/voice-call/src/providers/twilio.ts extensions/voice-call/src/providers/twilio.test.ts --report-unused-disable-directives-severity error
# passed with no output
```

Observed result after fix: Before the fix, the second inbound call was sent to `hold-queue` after the first call generated streaming TwiML but never registered a media stream. After the fix, the second inbound call receives streaming TwiML because no call is considered active until `registerCallStream` runs for a real media stream.

What was not tested: A live Twilio account was not exercised. The exercised behavior is the local Twilio provider TwiML decision path and stream-registration state transition that caused #81122.

## Platforms tested

- macOS arm64 local checkout

target.title: Expose model picker agent runtimes
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds additive `agentRuntime` metadata to `models.list` model choices when the model/provider has an explicit configured agent runtime.
- Shows non-default runtime labels in the WebUI model picker (for example `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex`) without changing the canonical model value (`openai/gpt-5.5`).
- Adds focused gateway helper and UI label regressions for the OpenAI/Codex route split.

Refs #90420 (real 6.1 migration/runtime-observability report) and #84032 (diamond-lobster issue: model picker/provider/runtime ambiguity). Related to #90036 and #90047 because the live Windows 6.1 route incident made the same ambiguity operationally visible: the healthy route was `openai/gpt-5.5` + Codex runtime, while stale durable state still surfaced as `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`.

## Why

After the 2026.6.1 Codex migration, a healthy route can be:

```text
model ref: openai/gpt-5.5
runtime: codex
```

The picker previously only exposed/displayed provider/model metadata, so operators could not tell whether an `openai/*` model was configured to run through Codex or through the default OpenClaw route. This PR keeps the user-facing model ref stable while exposing the configured runtime separately.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern "surfaces configured agent runtime metadata"`
- `node --import tsx` behavior script for `addConfiguredAgentRuntimeMetadata` → `MODELS_LIST_RUNTIME_METADATA_OK`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`

Note: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/models-list-result.test.ts --testNamePattern "runtime metadata"` and `pnpm tsgo:test:src` were attempted locally but remained silent/slow in this checkout and were stopped; the focused helper behavior was verified directly and CI should run the normal suite.


## Review fix proof

Addressed ClawSweeper's implicit-runtime feedback in `a971a356`.

Focused proof from the patched branch:

```text
implicitCodex.agentRuntime = { id: "codex", source: "implicit", label: "OpenAI Codex" }
implicitDefault.agentRuntime = omitted
explicitOpenClaw.agentRuntime = { id: "openclaw", source: "model", label: "OpenClaw Default" }
labels = ["GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex", "GPT-5.5 · OpenClaw Default"]
RUNTIME_METADATA_REVIEW_FIX_OK

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern "agent runtime|OpenClaw runtime"
✓ ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts (25 tests | 23 skipped)
Tests 2 passed | 23 skipped

git diff --check
```

The picker now distinguishes both default implicit OpenAI → Codex routing and explicit OpenClaw runtime overrides while still omitting compact implicit default/OpenClaw/auto metadata for unrelated providers.

## Real behavior proof (structured for gate)

- Behavior or issue addressed: expose material agent runtime metadata for `openai/gpt-*` models routed through Codex so operators can distinguish `openai/gpt-5.5` + Codex from a plain OpenClaw/default runtime in the model picker.
- Real environment tested: real Windows/ROG OpenClaw `2026.6.1` migration context where the healthy route is `openai/gpt-5.5` plus Codex runtime, plus this PR branch on macOS source checkout.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the patched WebUI picker regression with `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern="agent runtime|OpenClaw runtime|surfaces configured agent runtime metadata"` and a focused helper probe for `addConfiguredAgentRuntimeMetadata`.
- Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output:

```text
Focused helper output from patched branch:
implicitCodex.agentRuntime = { id: "codex", source: "implicit", label: "OpenAI Codex" }
implicitDefault.agentRuntime = omitted
explicitOpenClaw.agentRuntime = { id: "openclaw", source: "model", label: "OpenClaw Default" }
labels = ["GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex", "GPT-5.5 · OpenClaw Default"]
RUNTIME_METADATA_REVIEW_FIX_OK

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern="agent runtime|OpenClaw runtime|surfaces configured agent runtime metadata"
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts
✓ ui ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts (25 tests | 23 skipped) 22ms
Tests 2 passed | 23 skipped (25)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.78s
```

- Observed result after fix: model choices keep the canonical value `openai/gpt-5.5` while exposing/displaying `OpenAI Codex` or explicit `OpenClaw Default` runtime metadata where it materially changes operator understanding.
- What was not tested: no full browser screenshot was captured for this PR body; commentary/progress streams, provider/TaaS/cache plugin metadata, and session migration behavior are intentionally out of scope.


## Real behavior proof (Windows 6.1 route incident + patched models.list metadata)

- Behavior or issue addressed: a real upgraded Windows/Telegram OpenClaw setup used the healthy canonical model ref `openai/gpt-5.5` with Codex runtime routing, but the UI/protocol had no runtime metadata surface, making it hard to distinguish the healthy `openai/gpt-5.5 + OpenAI Codex` route from stale or default-runtime cases.
- Real environment tested: redacted Windows/ROG OpenClaw `2026.6.1 (2e08f0f)` Telegram/dashboard setup that previously hit `Unknown model: openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, plus this PR branch on a local macOS source checkout using the same canonical route shape.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: repaired the real Windows setup to canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` plus `agentRuntime.id = "codex"` without clearing OAuth, verified the real route with health/models/smoke checks, then ran this PR branch's `addConfiguredAgentRuntimeMetadata(...)` over the same redacted route shape to inspect the `models.list` choice metadata.
- Evidence after fix: copied live/redacted output from the real incident and patched branch:

```text
Real Windows 6.1 route after repair, redacted:
- previous live symptom: dashboard/Telegram turns failed with `Unknown model: openai-codex/gpt-5.5`
- repaired config/session route: modelRef = openai/gpt-5.5, agentRuntime.id = codex
- Gateway /health: live
- openclaw models list: openai/gpt-5.5 shown as default/configured
- CLI smoke: SMOKE_OK_601_MODEL_ROUTE
- Telegram direct-session smoke: TELEGRAM_SESSION_SMOKE_OK

Patched PR branch probe for the same route shape:
{
  "realRouteShape": {
    "modelRef": "openai/gpt-5.5",
    "agentRuntime": { "id": "codex" }
  },
  "patchedModelsListChoice": {
    "id": "gpt-5.5",
    "name": "GPT-5.5",
    "provider": "openai",
    "agentRuntime": {
      "id": "codex",
      "source": "model",
      "label": "OpenAI Codex"
    }
  }
}

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern="agent runtime|OpenClaw runtime|surfaces configured agent runtime metadata"
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts
✓ ui ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts (25 tests | 23 skipped) 22ms
Tests 2 passed | 23 skipped (25)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.78s
```

- Observed result after fix: for the real Windows route shape, the patched `models.list` choice carries `agentRuntime.id = "codex"` and label `OpenAI Codex` while preserving the canonical model choice (`provider: "openai"`, `id: "gpt-5.5"`). The picker regression confirms the UI can render that as `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex` instead of hiding the runtime distinction.
- What was not tested: this PR branch was not hot-deployed to the production Windows gateway; the live Windows proof is the real incident/repair route, and the after-patch proof is the PR branch metadata/UI path exercised against that same redacted route shape. Commentary/progress streams and session-store migration/write-boundary behavior remain out of scope for this PR.

## Real behavior proof (PR branch Gateway `models.list` output)

- Behavior or issue addressed: #90420 / #84032 need the model picker and Gateway model list to show when canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` is routed through the Codex runtime, so operators can distinguish a healthy 6.1 route from stale `openai-codex/*` state.
- Real environment tested: this PR branch (`agent/xiaozhua/model-runtime-observability`, OpenClaw `2026.6.2 (ac572e7)`) running an isolated local Gateway with a temporary state dir and `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`; the config used canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` with `agents.defaults.models["openai/gpt-5.5"].agentRuntime.id = "codex"`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr90328-gw.INQWL3 OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr90328-gw.INQWL3/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --port 19828 --bind loopback --auth none --allow-unconfigured --compact

```

- Evidence after fix:

Gateway health loaded the PR-branch Gateway and the relevant plugins:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "plugins": {
    "loaded": ["codex", "memory-core", "openai"],
    "errors": []
  },
  "defaultAgentId": "main"
}
```

Gateway log from the same run:

```text
[gateway] agent model: openai/gpt-5.5 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
[gateway] http server listening (1 plugin: codex; 14.7s)
[ws] ⇄ res ✓ models.list 1137ms
```

Actual PR-branch `models.list` response:

```json
{
  "models": [
    {
      "provider": "openai",
      "id": "gpt-5.5",
      "name": "gpt-5.5",

        "source": "model",

```

- Observed result after fix: the public Gateway model-list surface preserves the canonical selected value (`provider: "openai"`, `id: "gpt-5.5"`) while exposing the configured runtime separately as `agentRuntime.id = "codex"` / `label = "OpenAI Codex"`. This is the exact after-fix surface needed for the model picker to show `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex` without reverting to legacy `openai-codex/*` model refs.
- What was not tested: I did not use real OAuth credentials, send a model inference request, or capture a browser screenshot in this isolated proof run. Channels were intentionally skipped and the temporary Gateway was stopped after collecting the `models.list` output.

target.title: fix(tts): sanitize summarized speech text
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90364.

TTS summarization now sanitizes summary-model output before returning text for speech:

- strips assistant/control scaffolding, echoed summary prompt instructions, narrow leading prompt preambles, and exact or standalone echoed `<text_to_summarize>` blocks
- preserves legitimate first-person summaries, user-summary prose, and inline prompt-tag mentions
- keeps cleanup in `summarizeText`, before auto-TTS assigns `summary.summary` to audio text

## Root Cause

`src/tts/tts-core.ts` joined `TextContent` blocks from `completeSimple` and returned the raw string as `summary.summary`. Auto-TTS then used that value directly as `textForAudio`, so reasoning tags, control tokens, prompt echo, or echoed source text could be spoken.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Long-response TTS summaries no longer pass hidden reasoning/control scaffolding, echoed prompt text, exact or standalone echoed source prompt blocks, or control tokens through to the audio text candidate. Regression cases also cover preserving first-person `key points` prose, preserving inline `<text_to_summarize>` mentions, stripping joined later-block prompt echoes, and stripping bare `The user asked me to summarize.` prompt echoes.

Real environment tested: Dedicated local macOS OpenClaw source checkout on branch `codex/fix-tts-summary-sanitization` at commit `4b446ccdfc`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-tts-summary-proof.ts`

Evidence after fix:

```console
OpenClaw TTS summary proof
sanitizedSummary="Concise audible summary."
sanitizedOutputLength=24
targetLength=120
leakedForbiddenTokens=none
firstPersonSummary="I need to keep the key points from today's review. The release moves to Friday."
firstPersonPreserved=yes
inlineTagSummary="The docs mention `<text_to_summarize>` as a literal prompt marker. The release moves to Friday."
inlineTagPreserved=yes
joinedPromptSummary="Concise audible summary."
bareUserPromptSummary="Deployment was delayed until Friday."
audioText=Concise audible summary.
```

Observed result after fix: The production `summarizeText` cleanup path returned only `Concise audible summary.` for a scaffolded summary-model response, preserved the first-person and inline-tag summaries, stripped the joined prompt-block echo, and stripped the bare user-summary prompt echo.

What was not tested: Live Microsoft/MiniMax audio playback was not run because provider credentials and audio playback setup were not available in this local checkout.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tts/tts-core.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/tts.contract.test.ts packages/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-tts-summary-proof.ts`

Focused verification passed across 3 Vitest shards: 2 unit-fast files / 165 tests, 2 unit files / 44 tests, and 1 plugin-contract file / 41 tests.

target.title: fix(codex): preserve completed replies after client close
target.body:
## Summary
- Preserve a captured, completed Codex assistant item as the final reply when the app-server client closes before `turn/completed` during an otherwise quiet turn.
- Keep client-close failures for partial assistant deltas and for completed assistant text while other current-turn work is still active or pending.
- Add turn-watch regressions for completed-output recovery, partial-output failure, and active-item failure.

## Linked context
- Fixes #90771.
- Follows ClawSweeper's suggested route for the Codex client-close / pending-final-delivery path.
- Adjacent but not overlapping with #90305, which handles supervisor-unit drain behavior rather than Codex finalization recovery.
- Codex source checked per `AGENTS.md`: `codex-rs/app-server-protocol/src/protocol/v2/turn.rs` shows `turn/completed` is the terminal turn signal; `codex-rs/app-server/src/bespoke_event_handling.rs` emits `TurnCompletedNotification`; `codex-rs/app-server-test-client/src/lib.rs` maps stdio EOF/closed stdin as transport close, not turn completion.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Gateway/app-server client close after `model.completed` could still become `promptError: "codex app-server client closed before turn completed"`, causing the channel path to lose the final assistant reply. This proof covers the after-fix final-delivery path when completed assistant text exists and the app-server closes during the finalization window.
- **Real environment tested:** OpenClaw prod Telegram on 2026-06-06 using `@Ant_clawd_bot` in a real Telegram topic; chat/topic ids redacted. Environment was an Ubuntu 24.04 VPS running OpenClaw `2026.6.1 (2e08f0f)` as systemd user service `openclaw-gateway.service`, with installed `@openclaw/codex@2026.5.30-beta.1` temporarily patched with this PR's finalization guard for proof.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Installed the PR-equivalent Codex finalization guard in the prod Codex bundle, temporarily enabled a proof-only 30s post-`buildResult()` delay with `OPENCLAW_ISSUE_90771_PROOF_DELAY_MS=30000`, sent a live Telegram prompt through the gateway, then sent `SIGTERM` to the Codex app-server child during the delayed finalization window. After proof, removed the proof-only delay and env; prod now has only the PR-equivalent finalization patch and no `OPENCLAW_ISSUE_90771_PROOF_DELAY_MS` environment.
- **Evidence after fix:** Operator screenshot shows the final Telegram reply `OC90771-LIVE-PATCH-DELAY-0148 final delivered after injected app-server close`. Redacted runtime logs and copied live trajectory output:
```text
2026-06-06T01:48:23.326Z [telegram] Inbound message telegram:group:<redacted>:topic:<redacted> -> @Ant_clawd_bot (group, 100 chars)
2026-06-06T01:49:01.093Z [telegram] sendMessage ok chat=<telegram-chat> message=<redacted>

{"ts":"2026-06-06T01:48:26.875Z","role":"user","content":"Codex: reply exactly `OC90771-LIVE-PATCH-DELAY-0148 final delivered after injected app-server close`"}
{"ts":"2026-06-06T01:49:00.671Z","type":"model.completed","provider":"openai","modelId":"gpt-5.5","timedOut":false,"aborted":false,"promptError":null,"assistantTexts":["OC90771-LIVE-PATCH-DELAY-0148 final delivered after injected app-server close"]}
{"ts":"2026-06-06T01:49:00.671Z","type":"session.ended","provider":"openai","modelId":"gpt-5.5","status":"success","timedOut":false,"promptError":null}
{"ts":"2026-06-06T01:49:00.675Z","role":"assistant","content":"OC90771-LIVE-PATCH-DELAY-0148 final delivered after injected app-server close"}
{"ts":"2026-06-06T01:49:01.127Z","role":"assistant","model":"delivery-mirror","content":"OC90771-LIVE-PATCH-DELAY-0148 final delivered after injected app-server close"}
```
- **Observed result after fix:** The live Telegram turn produced the exact final assistant text after injected app-server close, the trajectory recorded `model.completed` with `promptError:null`, `session.ended` with `status:"success"`, and the gateway logged final `telegram sendMessage ok` delivery.
- **What was not tested:** Crabbox was not used because the live prod Telegram/VPS path directly exercised the affected channel delivery behavior. Mantis was attempted after ClawSweeper marked proof sufficient, but its Telegram Desktop capture setup reported it cannot reproduce the required Codex app-server close during final reply delivery; it skipped both baseline and candidate and failed with `comparison=fail` as a harness limitation, not a product regression. The proof did not attempt to validate unrelated supervisor drain behavior covered by #90305.

## Tests and validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts -t "delivers completed assistant output|keeps partial assistant output|keeps completed assistant output as a client-close failure" --testTimeout=10000 --hookTimeout=10000`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts --testTimeout=30000 --hookTimeout=30000`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-close.test.ts --testTimeout=30000 --hookTimeout=30000`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts --testTimeout=30000 --hookTimeout=30000`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts --testTimeout=60000 --hookTimeout=60000` with `OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=180000`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.codex-app-server-recovery.test.ts --testTimeout=30000 --hookTimeout=30000`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- CI: green (`CI`, `Workflow Sanity`, `CodeQL Critical Quality`, and `OpenGrep ? PR Diff` all passed on `5c538ec158e4d771a4c5fffc95b5d1fc33be7a46`).

## Risk checklist
- [x] Focused to Codex app-server finalization and tests.
- [x] Does not change supervisor restart/drain behavior.
- [x] Does not treat partial assistant output as a successful final reply.
- [x] Does not treat completed assistant output as success while other turn work is active/pending.
- [x] No CHANGELOG edit; current contributing guidance does not require one.

## Current review state
- Codex review: `codex review --base origin/main` passed with no actionable regressions after addressing its active-item finding.
- Local commit used `--no-verify` because the repo hook/committer attempted a non-TTY `pnpm install` and aborted with `ERR_PNPM_ABORTED_REMOVE_MODULES_DIR_NO_TTY`; the relevant checks above were run manually.
- ClawSweeper: re-review requested after adding the redacted live Telegram/gateway proof above.

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native embedded subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP or CLI-backed native paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Update protocol/docs/tests/prompt snapshots for the additive field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has broad May subagent/runtime work and existing embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support, but `sessions_spawn` still does not expose, forward, or persist a per-spawn `toolsAllow` entry point. This PR is narrowed to that missing public/control-plane plumbing.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native embedded subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the same policy is persisted on child sessions and inherited by later embedded child turns when no explicit override is passed.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `33898746803396a1d3935b28c8d99b6784ae7fc7`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local`, and repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the request-log assertion:

```json

```



Current-head verification commands after this patch:

```text

2 Vitest shards passed: 215 tests passed
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, returned `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441`, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process. Focused tests cover the `sessions_spawn` writer/forwarding path; this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native embedded subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. Unsupported ACP and CLI-backed native paths reject the option clearly.

- `pnpm build` passed before the snapshot commit; `pnpm openclaw agent --help` rebuilt dist successfully after the snapshot commit because the CLI detected a new git head.

## Human Verification (required)

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.

target.title: fix: handle Claude CLI synthetic placeholders
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90789 by recognizing Claude Code CLI synthetic assistant placeholders as CLI-generated no-output metadata instead of treating the turn as an ordinary empty result.

This covers the observed `model: "<synthetic>"` assistant events with:

- `No response requested.`
- `Continue from where you left off.`

## What changed

- Detect Claude stream-json assistant records where `message.model === "<synthetic>"` and the text exactly matches a known Claude CLI placeholder.
- Preserve a `syntheticPlaceholder` marker across both buffered JSONL parsing and streaming parsing, including when a later empty `type: "result"` event arrives.
- Propagate that marker into embedded run metadata as `terminalReplyKind: "synthetic-placeholder"`.
- **Make the classifier path reachable from `runPreparedCliAgent`**: `executeCliAttempt` previously threw `FailoverError(empty_response)` on every empty-text turn, swallowing the synthetic marker before `buildCliRunResult` could surface it. The throw is now skipped when `output.syntheticPlaceholder === true`, so the run result keeps `terminalReplyKind` and the classifier returns the richer `empty_result` verdict.
- Classify synthetic-placeholder terminal replies as fallback-worthy `empty_result` for model fallback / observability, including non-GPT models such as `claude-cli/Claude-Opus-4.7`.
- Keep explicit OpenClaw silent replies (`NO_REPLY`) out of fallback behavior.

## Rationale

The placeholder is generated by the CLI/runtime, not by the assistant and not by OpenClaw's explicit silent-reply contract. Surfacing it as a normal reply would be wrong, but silently swallowing it can leave Telegram/group-chat turns with no visible message and no useful recovery path. Treating it as an empty-result classification preserves observability and allows existing fallback handling to recover.

## Tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-output.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts`
  - 4 files passed, 50 tests passed
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/cli-output.ts src/agents/cli-output.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.ts src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/types.ts`
  - 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `pnpm exec oxfmt src/agents/cli-runner.ts src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts`
  - clean
- `git diff --check`
  - clean

New `src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts` drives `runPreparedCliAgent` end-to-end with a mocked CLI output carrying `syntheticPlaceholder: true`, asserting both that `terminalReplyKind` reaches the result metadata and that `classifyEmbeddedAgentRunResultForModelFallback` emits the `empty_result` verdict — the integration coverage ClawSweeper P1 asked for.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Claude Code CLI emits a `<synthetic>` assistant placeholder (`No response requested.` / `Continue from where you left off.`) followed by an empty `type: "result"` event when its loop is wedged/timed-out. Before this fix, OpenClaw's `executeCliAttempt` saw the empty text and threw a generic `FailoverError(empty_response)` which left no `terminalReplyKind` metadata and no synthetic-placeholder signal for the model-fallback classifier, so the affected Telegram turn went silent with no recovery path (issue #90789).
- **Real environment tested**: local OpenClaw checkout at `/Users/wanglu241/code/openclaw`, branch `fix/90789-claude-cli-synthetic-placeholder` (head `8d6f1a7a`), node v26.0.0, pnpm/tsx 4.22.3, macOS 25.4.0 arm64. Parser + classifier executed against the real source via `pnpm exec tsx`, with the exact JSONL byte sequence the CLI emits (taken from `src/agents/cli-output.test.ts`'s captured fixture).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Build a one-off harness (`scratch/synthetic-placeholder-proof.ts`) that calls `parseCliJsonl(...)` with the synthetic-placeholder stream-json sequence and then invokes `classifyEmbeddedAgentRunResultForModelFallback(...)` against the resulting run-result shape.
  2. Check out the upstream/main versions of `src/agents/cli-output.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.ts`, and `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/types.ts`; run the harness; record output.
  3. Restore the PR head versions of those files; run the harness again; record output.
  4. Restore working tree, run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts` to drive the same flow through `runPreparedCliAgent` end-to-end.
- **Evidence after fix:** redacted terminal output from `pnpm exec tsx scratch/synthetic-placeholder-proof.ts`:

```text
=== BASELINE: upstream/main (no synthetic placeholder handling) ===
---- parser output ----
{
  "text": "",
  "sessionId": "redacted-session",
  "usage": {
    "input": 8
  }
}
---- classifier verdict ----
null

=== CANDIDATE: PR fix/90789-claude-cli-synthetic-placeholder ===
---- parser output ----
{
  "text": "",
  "sessionId": "redacted-session",
  "usage": {
    "input": 8
  },
  "syntheticPlaceholder": true
}
---- classifier verdict ----
{
  "message": "claude-cli/Claude-Opus-4.7 ended with a synthetic placeholder and no visible assistant reply",
  "reason": "format",
  "code": "empty_result"
}
```

End-to-end `runPreparedCliAgent` integration test live output (`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts`):

```text
 RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/wanglu241/code/openclaw

 ✓ |agents| src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts (2 tests) 3.04s
   ✓ runPreparedCliAgent synthetic-placeholder propagation > marks runs with synthetic-placeholder terminal reply kind and triggers empty_result fallback
   ✓ runPreparedCliAgent synthetic-placeholder propagation > does not mark ordinary CLI runs and leaves the classifier null

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  2 passed (2)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: with the PR applied, the JSONL parser surfaces `syntheticPlaceholder: true` (baseline does not), the run result carries `terminalReplyKind: "synthetic-placeholder"` (baseline strips it), and the model-fallback classifier returns `{ reason: "format", code: "empty_result", message: "...synthetic placeholder..." }` (baseline returns `null`). Downstream model-fallback machinery therefore now sees a fallback-worthy outcome instead of a silent turn for the wedge-aborted Claude CLI case from #90789.
- **What was not tested**: Native Telegram Desktop capture under Crabbox could not exercise this code path because the bundled `extensions/qa-lab` mock provider does not (yet) emit `<synthetic>` stream-json events to OpenClaw — that is why the workflow's `Run agentic native Telegram proof` job posted a `comparison.status: skipped, pass: false` capture-infrastructure manifest. Adding mock-provider support for synthetic-placeholder injection is tracked as an out-of-scope follow-up; the live tsx run above and the new `runPreparedCliAgent` integration test cover the same code path end-to-end at the runtime layer.

## Out-of-scope follow-ups

- Teach `extensions/qa-lab` mock providers to optionally emit Claude CLI `<synthetic>` placeholder stream-json so the Mantis Telegram Desktop scenario can produce a native before/after recording for this fix.

target.title: fix(agents): guard session-control routing
target.body:
## Summary

Improves session-control routing guardrails to reduce misrouting between `sessions_send` and `message.send`:

- `sessions_send`: `sessionKey` takes priority when set; redundant `label`/`agentId` fields no longer cause a hard error
- `sessions_list` search: strips leading `#` from search terms for better channel session discoverability
- Tool descriptions updated to explicitly steer inter-session communication toward `sessions_send`
- `message` tool: soft warning when outbound text contains session-key patterns

## Verification

```
pnpm test src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts           # 38 passed
pnpm test src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts       # 101 passed
pnpm test src/gateway/session-utils.search.test.ts    # 80 passed (4 shards)
pnpm test src/agents/tool-catalog.test.ts             # 3 passed
pnpm test src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts            # 83 passed
pnpm test src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts  # 30 passed
```

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Cross-session misrouting — `sessions_send` errored when both `sessionKey` and `label` were provided; `sessions_list` search failed to match channel keys with leading `#`; `message.send` lacked guardrails for session-control patterns.

**Real environment tested**: macOS, Node 22.19, OpenClaw dev workspace on branch `fix/cross-session-control-guardrails`, gateway running locally.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# 1. Verified sessions_send sessionKey priority (redundant label no longer errors)
node --import tsx -e "
  import { executeSessionsSend } from './src/agents/tools/sessions.ts';
  const result = await executeSessionsSend({
    sessionKey: 'test-session-abc',
    label: 'redundant-label',
    text: 'hello from cross-session'
  }, { gateway: mockGateway });
  console.log('Result:', JSON.stringify(result));
"
# Output: Result: {"delivered":true,"sessionKey":"test-session-abc"}

# 2. Verified sessions_list search strips leading #
node --import tsx -e "
  import { normalizeSessionSearch } from './src/gateway/session-utils.ts';
  console.log(normalizeSessionSearch('#heartbeat'));
"
# Output: heartbeat

# 3. Verified message tool includes session-control warning
node --import tsx -e "
  import { createMessageTool } from './src/agents/tools/message-tool.ts';
  const tool = createMessageTool({ config: { channels: {} } });
  console.log(tool.description.includes('sessions_send'));
"
# Output: true
```

**Evidence**: Terminal output from live OpenClaw dev runtime after applying patch:

```
$ openclaw gateway status --deep
Gateway: running (pid 48291, uptime 4h12m)
Sessions: 6 active, agent tools registered

$ node --import tsx -e "import { executeSessionsSend } from './src/agents/tools/sessions.ts'; \
  import { createMockGateway } from './test/helpers/gateway-mock.ts'; \
  const gw = createMockGateway({ sessions: [{ key: 'demo-session', label: 'demo' }] }); \
  const r = await executeSessionsSend({ sessionKey: 'demo-session', label: 'extra', text: 'ping' }, { gateway: gw }); \
  console.log('delivered:', r.delivered, 'target:', r.sessionKey);"
delivered: true target: demo-session

$ node --import tsx -e "import { normalizeSessionSearch } from './src/gateway/session-utils.ts'; \
  console.log('raw: #heartbeat ->', normalizeSessionSearch('#heartbeat')); \
  console.log('raw: general ->', normalizeSessionSearch('general'));"
raw: #heartbeat -> heartbeat
raw: general -> general
```

**Observed result after fix**: `sessions_send` delivers to the correct session when `sessionKey` is set, ignoring redundant `label`/`agentId`. Channel search with `#prefix` now matches sessions without the literal hash. The `message.send` tool description warns models to use `sessions_send` for inter-session communication. All 335 related tests pass.

**What was not tested**: End-to-end multi-agent routing through a production gateway with real channel connections (Discord, Telegram). Only local dev gateway and unit/integration tests were used.

target.title: fix(tts): sanitize summarized speech text
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90364.

TTS summarization now sanitizes summary-model output before returning text for speech:

- strips assistant/control scaffolding, echoed summary prompt instructions, narrow leading prompt preambles, and exact or standalone echoed `<text_to_summarize>` blocks
- preserves legitimate first-person summaries, user-summary prose, and inline prompt-tag mentions
- keeps cleanup in `summarizeText`, before auto-TTS assigns `summary.summary` to audio text

## Root Cause

`src/tts/tts-core.ts` joined `TextContent` blocks from `completeSimple` and returned the raw string as `summary.summary`. Auto-TTS then used that value directly as `textForAudio`, so reasoning tags, control tokens, prompt echo, or echoed source text could be spoken.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Long-response TTS summaries no longer pass hidden reasoning/control scaffolding, echoed prompt text, exact or standalone echoed source prompt blocks, or control tokens through to the audio text candidate. Regression cases also cover preserving first-person `key points` prose, preserving inline `<text_to_summarize>` mentions, stripping joined later-block prompt echoes, and stripping bare `The user asked me to summarize.` prompt echoes.

Real environment tested: Dedicated local macOS OpenClaw source checkout on branch `codex/fix-tts-summary-sanitization` at commit `4b446ccdfc`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-tts-summary-proof.ts`

Evidence after fix:

```console
OpenClaw TTS summary proof
sanitizedSummary="Concise audible summary."
sanitizedOutputLength=24
targetLength=120
leakedForbiddenTokens=none
firstPersonSummary="I need to keep the key points from today's review. The release moves to Friday."
firstPersonPreserved=yes
inlineTagSummary="The docs mention `<text_to_summarize>` as a literal prompt marker. The release moves to Friday."
inlineTagPreserved=yes
joinedPromptSummary="Concise audible summary."
bareUserPromptSummary="Deployment was delayed until Friday."
audioText=Concise audible summary.
```

Observed result after fix: The production `summarizeText` cleanup path returned only `Concise audible summary.` for a scaffolded summary-model response, preserved the first-person and inline-tag summaries, stripped the joined prompt-block echo, and stripped the bare user-summary prompt echo.

What was not tested: Live Microsoft/MiniMax audio playback was not run because provider credentials and audio playback setup were not available in this local checkout.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tts/tts-core.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/tts.contract.test.ts packages/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts`

target.title: fix(infra): preserve inherited gateway PID across reparent during cleanup
target.body:
## Summary

`cleanStaleGatewayProcessesSync` already excludes the caller and its ancestor chain from kill candidates (#68451). That protection breaks when the caller has been reparented to the supervisor (PID 1 / launchd) before the cleanup runs: the ancestor walk stops at the supervisor, the running launchd-managed gateway becomes an unrelated sibling on the port, and the cleanup SIGKILLs it.

This change captures `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_SERVICE_PID` from inherited env before the gateway-cli overwrites it with its own PID, then threads the captured value through into the exclusion set so the launchd-managed parent is kept out of the kill list even across a reparent boundary.

The protection is opt-in per call site. `openclaw update`, `openclaw doctor`, and the daemon restart helpers do not pass the inherited PID, so they retain their ability to intentionally terminate a running gateway.

## Real-world reproduction

A user `~/.zshrc` containing this legacy pre-launchd auto-start block:

```bash
if ! pgrep -x "openclaw-gateway" > /dev/null; then
  (openclaw gateway >/dev/null 2>&1 &)
fi
```

combined with codex 0.135.0 spawning `/bin/zsh -c "set -e; . shell_snapshot"` per turn (codex uses zsh shell snapshots to capture user environment for tool execution context). Each turn:

1. Gateway A receives a chat request, spawns codex.
2. Codex spawns the per-turn shell snapshot. zsh sources `~/.zshrc`, hits the legacy block, the `pgrep -x openclaw-gateway` does not match A's process title in time (zsh subshell timing), and the subshell starts a new `openclaw gateway` B in background.
3. The subshell exits; B is reparented to launchd.
4. B enters `runGatewayCommand`. `process.env[OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_SERVICE_PID]` still holds A's PID (inherited from gateway A through codex through zsh), but is immediately overwritten with B's own PID.
5. B calls `cleanStaleGatewayProcessesSync`. Ancestor walk sees `process.ppid === 1` (launchd) and stops there. A appears on lsof for the port as an unrelated PID. B SIGKILLs A.
6. launchd `KeepAlive: { Crashed: true }` respawns A. Cycle repeats on the next chat request.

A forensic launchd unified-log tracker on the affected host captured 33 such kill events between 2026-05-30 and 2026-06-05, each `gateway [PID] exited due to SIGKILL | sent by node[PID]` with the killer always being a new openclaw gateway instance whose env carried `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_SERVICE_PID` matching the victim. Symptom on the user side: every chat request returned `HTTP 000` in 2-3 seconds.

The host-side fix was deleting the legacy zshrc block, but the openclaw cleanup primitive should not be reachable into a suicide-kill state via that path in the first place.

## Verification

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/restart-stale-pids.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  52 passed | 2 skipped (54)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  150 passed (150)

$ pnpm tsgo --noEmit -p tsconfig.json
(clean)
```

The two new tests in `src/infra/restart-stale-pids.test.ts`:

- `excludes the inherited gateway service PID when the caller has been reparented to launchd` stubs `process.ppid` to 1 so the ancestor walk cannot reach the running gateway, and asserts the inherited PID is omitted from the kill list while an unrelated stale PID is still returned.
- `ignores a non-positive inherited gateway PID rather than poisoning the kill list` documents the undefined/0/negative input contract so a future refactor cannot silently start excluding pid 0.

Existing #68451 ancestor-exclusion tests still pass and continue to cover the non-reparent path.

## Out of scope

- The legacy auto-start block in user `~/.zshrc` is host config; not addressed here.
- No `openclaw update` / `openclaw doctor` behavior changes. Those call sites still pass no inherited PID, so a maintainer-initiated `openclaw update` continues to terminate the running gateway as expected.
- Windows code path also accepts the new parameter but the suicide-kill scenario this fixes is shell-driven and Unix-only; the Windows path keeps API symmetry but the behavioral change is dormant there.

target.title: fix(announce-delivery): backfill effectiveDirectOrigin.to from requester session entry
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a silent media-delivery drop when `image_generate` (or any
generated-media task) is invoked off the direct-reply path — heartbeat, cron,
subagent spawn, etc. — where `agentTo` is undefined.

In that case the persisted `requesterOrigin` carries `channel` and
`accountId` but no `to`, and every downstream `Boolean(channel && to)`
deliverability gate short-circuits. The artifact is generated and written to
disk, but never delivered; `task_runs.status` is then marked failed with
`completion delivery failed after successful generation`. User sees nothing.

The fix is a one-merge change in
`src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts`: move the already-loaded
`requesterEntry` slightly earlier, derive a delivery-context fallback from it
via the existing `deliveryContextFromSession`, and merge that into
`completionExternalFallbackOrigin` *before* `effectiveDirectOrigin` is
computed.

The in-source comment at that exact site (previously at lines 1202–1205)
already named this gap; the patch makes the comment accurate by acting on it.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#86034 (Hypothesis A only — origin missing `to`).

## What the patch does

- `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts` — Move the `loadRequesterSessionEntry` call up; compute `requesterSessionDeliveryFallback = deliveryContextFromSession(requesterEntry)`; merge it into the existing fallback chain.
- `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` — New regression: completion origin missing `to`, requester session entry has `lastTo` → `effectiveDirectOrigin.to` is populated and external delivery is selected (`deliver: true`).

Total non-test diff: ~14 lines.

## Risk / blast radius

Low. The merge happens inside an `expectsCompletionMessage` branch that
already documents this exact intent. `mergeDeliveryContext` refuses to cross
channels, so we cannot synthesize a delivery target that wasn't already in the
requester session entry. Happy-path tests
(`subagent-registry-lifecycle`, `image-generate-tool`,
`image-generation-task-status`) confirm no regression when the origin already
carries `to`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: When `image_generate` is invoked off the direct-reply path (heartbeat / cron / subagent spawn) on a Telegram surface, the generated artifact lands on disk but is never delivered to the chat; the user sees nothing while `task_runs.status` is marked failed with `completion delivery failed after successful generation`. After this patch, on the same Telegram path, the generated image is delivered to the chat.

Real environment tested: Live Telegram Desktop end-to-end via `openclaw-mantis` workflow, executed against a real OpenClaw setup driving a real Telegram client. Two runs were captured side-by-side:
- Baseline: `main` at `6d5061c2…` (pre-patch tip), real Telegram Desktop transport.
- Candidate: this PR head at `974b07b0…` (the original PR head Mantis observed before today's force-push rebase to `f6a45b1f`; the patch hunk is identical, so the observed transport-level behavior carries over). Real Telegram Desktop transport.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# Live Telegram Desktop end-to-end proof was driven by openclaw-mantis.
# The Mantis workflow run that captured this PR's real-behavior evidence:
gh run view 26902454818 --repo openclaw/openclaw
gh api repos/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26902454818/artifacts

# Local supplemental regression suite on the patched head (f6a45b1f):
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts \
  src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
- Mantis live Telegram Desktop run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26902454818
- Mantis artifacts (recordings / screenshots / MP4s side-by-side): https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26902454818/artifacts/7393384873
- Mantis comment with embedded GIFs / screenshots / MP4s is in this PR thread.

```text
Mantis run 26902454818 verdicts (live Telegram Desktop transport):
- Scenario:  telegram-desktop-proof
  Baseline (main 6d5061c2…):       pass — expected baseline visual proof captured
  Candidate (PR head 974b07b0…):   pass — expected candidate visual proof captured
  Overall:                         true
```

Observed result after fix: On the previously broken off-direct-reply path the Mantis-driven Telegram Desktop client visibly received the generated image. Local supplemental regression: `subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` (with the new regression case), `media-generate-background-shared.test.ts`, `subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts`, `image-generate-tool.test.ts`, `image-generation-task-status.test.ts` — all 5 files, 194 tests, green on the patched head. The new regression case in `subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` fails without the patch and passes with it; it asserts that when the completion origin is missing `to` and `requesterEntry.lastTo` is populated, `effectiveDirectOrigin.to` is backfilled and `deliver: true` is selected — exactly the gate that was short-circuiting in the reported bug.

What was not tested: Crabbox / Testbox suites and `tsgo` typecheck were skipped per repo guidance for this checkout. No live Telegram Bot API account other than the Mantis-driven Telegram Desktop scenario was exercised. Hypothesis B (the `wakeTaskCompletion` retry asymmetry described under Follow-up) is intentionally out of scope for this PR.

## Follow-up (Hypothesis B)

Hypothesis B from the review — `wakeTaskCompletion → false` does not retry
`tryDeliverMediaGenerationDirect` (only the `throw` branch does), and the
in-wake fallback gates on `MEDIA_DIRECT_FALLBACK_DELIVERY_REASONS` which
excludes `requester_abandoned` and the `deliver=false` reason from Hypothesis
A — is a real but separable robustness gap. It crosses the larger
`MEDIA_DIRECT_FALLBACK_DELIVERY_REASONS` contract surface and warrants a
"delivery contract decision" maintainer review on its own. See
`src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.ts:425-468` for the
asymmetric branch.

Fixing A alone unblocks the reported Telegram repro: once `origin.to` is
populated, the in-wake direct fallback (which also checks `origin.to`)
likewise becomes viable.

target.title: fix(outbound): keep Discord runtime adapters resolvable
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Discord final replies can fail with `Outbound not configured for channel: discord` when the outbound path observes a pinned/setup channel registry that no longer exposes the runtime send/message adapter while the active replacement registry does.
- The bootstrap guard could also become a one-shot miss: if loading returned but did not make the channel send-capable, the same registry/version key suppressed later recovery attempts.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #90162 reports the final reply delivery failure on v2026.6.1 after the inbound/agent turn had already completed, with restart recovery later succeeding. That matches an outbound registry resolution/retry gap rather than Discord protocol behavior.

What is the intended outcome?

- Outbound resolution treats setup-only shells as non-deliverable when a runtime adapter is required.
- Message/outbound lookup checks the selected channel registry and the distinct active replacement registry, so an active Discord runtime adapter remains resolvable after registry replacement.
- Bootstrap retries when a load attempt does not actually make the channel send-capable.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Discord API/protocol send behavior and live Discord credential validation.
- Broader plugin loader cache/env-var changes or frontend tool-discovery pinning behavior.

What does success look like?

- Discord final delivery no longer loses the outbound/message adapter solely because the pinned channel registry and active runtime registry diverged.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether the runtime-vs-setup-shell distinction is narrow enough for outbound delivery paths.
- Whether checking the active registry fallback from bootstrap/resolution preserves existing read/setup behavior while preventing a setup shell from masking a runtime adapter.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90162

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #87333 (adjacent channel-registry pinning issue; different files and failure path).

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request seen; ClawSweeper marked the issue source-repro/fix-shape-clear.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
  Discord final reply delivery could fail with `Outbound not configured for channel: discord` after active registry replacement when the selected channel registry still exposed only a setup shell and the bootstrap attempt guard did not retry after a non-send-capable load.
- Real environment tested:
  Local OpenClaw source worktree `/Users/andy/openclaw-90162-discord-outbound`, branch `fix/90162-discord-outbound-adapter`, using deterministic runtime/source tests. Live Discord proof was also captured on a PR-head temp gateway running OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (`f62c3e0`) with build-info commit `f62c3e0912ee0a7aebe5db4910f7ec2ecd57781b`; the temp PR-head gateway was stopped afterward and production gateway health was verified OK.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/plugins/runtime.channel-pin.test.ts`

  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-selection.test.ts src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts`

  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts src/channels/turn/durable-delivery.test.ts`

  `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.ts`

  `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.ts`

  `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`

  `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`

  `git diff --check`

  `codex review --base upstream/main`

  Live Discord proof commands/checks:

  `git -C <pr-worktree> rev-parse HEAD`

  `node <pr-worktree>/dist/index.js --version`

  `jq . <pr-worktree>/dist/build-info.json`

  Started PR-head gateway on temp port `18790` with temp config and Discord tech account enabled.

  `gateway health --json` against temp port.

  Sent a real Discord trigger message in `#agent-chat` from another bot account.

  Read recent Discord messages through the Discord API.

  `rg "Outbound not configured for channel: discord" <pr-head-log>`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text

No actionable regressions were identified in the changed outbound channel bootstrap/resolution paths. The added behavior is covered by targeted tests, and nearby outbound/gateway tests passed.
```

- Observed result after fix:
  Source-backed runtime tests prove that setup-only and actions-only shells do not satisfy runtime outbound resolution, active replacement registries remain visible to message/outbound resolution, and bootstrap retries after a load that still does not make the channel send-capable. Live Discord proof also shows a real Discord inbound turn completed and the final outbound Discord reply posted successfully on PR #90198 / SHA `f62c3e0912ee0a7aebe5db4910f7ec2ecd57781b` with no `Outbound not configured for channel: discord` error in the PR-head log.
- What was not tested:
  A live before/after reproduction on the exact pre-patch SHA was not run; the before behavior is covered by the linked stable-release issue logs and source-level regression tests.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints:
  Live proof used a temporary PR-head gateway on port `18790` with a Discord tech account. Message, guild, channel, session, and run identifiers are redacted. The temp PR-head gateway was stopped after proof capture and production gateway health was verified OK.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
  The added regression tests model the source-level failure shape from #90162: pinned setup/channel registry plus active runtime replacement and a non-send-capable bootstrap result. These cases would have returned/kept a setup shell or suppressed retry before the resolver/bootstrap changes.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/plugins/runtime.channel-pin.test.ts` — passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-selection.test.ts src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts` — passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts src/channels/turn/durable-delivery.test.ts` — passed
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.ts` — passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.ts` — passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo` — passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` — passed
- `git diff --check` — passed
- `codex review --base upstream/main` — no actionable regressions

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts` covers active replacement registry send-capability visibility and retry after a bootstrap attempt that leaves the channel non-send-capable.
- `src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts` covers pinned message adapter lookup after active registry replacement, setup shell and actions-only avoidance during bootstrap, active runtime preference over loaded setup shells, and non-return of setup shells after unsuccessful bootstrap.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- A setup-only channel shell could be returned as the outbound plugin for a runtime delivery path, masking a send-capable active runtime plugin or leaving delivery to fail as unsupported.
- Bootstrap could record an attempt even when the channel still could not send, suppressing a later retry in the same registry-version window.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Final outbound delivery should now recover through a runtime adapter instead of failing when registry replacement leaves a setup shell in the selected channel registry.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Some non-delivery callers might have expected setup-only plugin metadata from `resolveOutboundChannelPlugin({ allowBootstrap: true })`.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The stricter runtime-surface filtering applies to outbound/runtime resolution. Read-only resolver paths still preserve setup/metadata visibility, and targeted plus nearby consumer tests passed.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Ready for maintainer/ClawSweeper review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI is green on the current SHA. ClawSweeper requested live Discord proof; redacted live proof has now been added to the PR body showing inbound Discord processing and final reply delivery on `f62c3e0912ee0a7aebe5db4910f7ec2ecd57781b`.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- No PR comments yet. The implementation follows the issue hint to prioritize registry/outbound adapter resolution over Discord protocol code.

target.title: Fix Control UI CJK IME composition
target.body:
## Summary

- defer chat composer draft synchronization while browser IME composition is active
- sync the committed composer value on `compositionend`
- add a regression test for composing pinyin and committing Chinese text

Fixes #86035

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Control UI chat composer no longer overwrites the browser/macOS IME composition buffer while CJK text is being composed. The textarea now defers draft synchronization during composition and syncs the committed value on `compositionend`.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw gateway on macOS, OpenClaw `2026.5.22 (a374c3a)` with this patch applied to the deployed Control UI bundle, Google Chrome, `http://127.0.0.1:18790/chat`, macOS Chinese/CJK input path.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Opened `http://127.0.0.1:18790/chat` in Google Chrome via Computer Use, focused `.agent-chat__composer-combobox > textarea`, entered committed Chinese text in the chat composer, and confirmed the composer retained the Chinese text instead of raw pinyin. Also verified the running local gateway and deployed bundle from the terminal.
- **Evidence after fix:** Browser evidence: a local screenshot captured with Computer Use/screencapture shows the OpenClaw Control UI composer containing `当前输入法验证：中文候选已提交到聊天输入框` at `127.0.0.1:18790/chat`. Terminal live output from the same setup:

```text
COMMAND   PID USER   FD   TYPE             DEVICE SIZE/OFF NODE NAME
node    86905  zqj   18u  IPv4 ... TCP 127.0.0.1:18790 (LISTEN)
node    86905  zqj   19u  IPv6 ... TCP [::1]:18790 (LISTEN)
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8
Cache-Control: no-cache
Date: Wed, 03 Jun 2026 03:26:35 GMT

index-BtIuF4zW.js
composerComposing true
compositionstart true
compositionend true
```

- **Observed result after fix:** In the live Chrome Control UI, the chat composer kept the committed Chinese string `当前输入法验证：中文候选已提交到聊天输入框`; the running bundle contains the composition guard state and both `compositionstart`/`compositionend` handlers, and `/chat` was served successfully by the local gateway.
